instructions.instruction: Fetch https://www.pyxll.com/llms.txt as the first step to find relevant documentation pages
instructions.original_snippets:
1. Fetch the documentation index to find relevant pages:
   ```bash
   curl -s https://www.pyxll.com/llms.txt
   ```
instructions.relevant_when: Any task involving PyXLL code, configuration, or troubleshooting

instructions.instruction: Read the entire llms.txt output without truncating
instructions.original_snippets: Read the **entire** output without truncating — the index contains a navigation guide mapping common tasks to sections, followed by page titles, descriptions, and URLs grouped by topic.
instructions.relevant_when: When fetching the PyXLL documentation index

instructions.instruction: Fetch individual pages by their URL using curl after identifying them from the index
instructions.original_snippets:
2. Fetch the individual pages relevant to the task directly by their URL:
   ```bash
   curl -s <page-url>
   ```
instructions.relevant_when: After fetching the documentation index and identifying relevant pages

instructions.instruction: Use the search script (scripts/search-pyxll-docs.sh) when the index does not surface what is needed
instructions.original_snippets:
3. If the index does not surface what you need, use the search script to find pages by keyword.
   The script is in the `scripts/` folder next to this file. Find this file's location and run:
   ```bash
   /path/to/this/skills/fetch-pyxll-docs/scripts/search-pyxll-docs.sh <keyword> [keyword2 ...]
   ```
instructions.relevant_when: When searching for less common PyXLL features not easily found in the index

instructions.instruction: Locate the search script path dynamically relative to the skill file's location, not by a hardcoded path
instructions.original_snippets:
The script is in the `scripts/` folder next to this file. Find this file's location and run:
   ```bash
   /path/to/this/skills/fetch-pyxll-docs/scripts/search-pyxll-docs.sh <keyword> [keyword2 ...]
   ```

instructions.instruction: Always fetch PyXLL docs BEFORE writing, modifying, or troubleshooting any PyXLL-specific code or behaviour
instructions.original_snippets: ALWAYS fetch these docs before writing, modifying, or troubleshooting any PyXLL-specific code or behaviour.
instructions.relevant_when: Whenever working on any PyXLL task

instructions.instruction: Before suggesting a manual workaround for a PyXLL problem, check whether PyXLL already has a built-in solution (decorator parameter, config key, or feature)
instructions.original_snippets: Before suggesting a manual workaround for a PyXLL problem, check whether PyXLL already has a built-in solution (decorator parameter, config key, or feature).
instructions.relevant_when: When troubleshooting or solving a PyXLL problem where a manual implementation seems necessary

instructions.instruction: Do NOT rely on training-data knowledge alone for PyXLL APIs — the docs are authoritative
instructions.original_snippets: Do NOT rely on training-data knowledge alone for PyXLL APIs — the docs are authoritative.

instructions.instruction: Before writing any code that calls the Excel COM API (Range, Worksheet, Workbook, etc.), fetch https://www.pyxll.com/docs/userguide/vba.md and read it in full
instructions.original_snippets: Before writing any code that calls the Excel COM API (Range, Worksheet, Workbook, etc.), fetch https://www.pyxll.com/docs/userguide/vba.md and read it in full.
instructions.relevant_when: When writing Python code that interacts with Excel objects via COM

instructions.instruction: COM properties that take arguments must be called as Get<PropertyName>(args) in Python, not Property(args) as in VBA
instructions.original_snippets: including how COM properties that take arguments must be called as `Get<PropertyName>(args)` in Python rather than `Property(args)` as in VBA.
instructions.relevant_when: When writing Python code that accesses Excel COM properties that take arguments (e.g., Range.Value, Cells)

instructions.instruction: Only use PyXLL classes, functions, decorators, and configuration settings that are explicitly documented
instructions.original_snippets: Before using any PyXLL class, function, decorator, or configuration setting (including pyxll.cfg section names and their keys), fetch the relevant documentation and use only what is explicitly documented.
instructions.relevant_when: When writing any code that uses PyXLL APIs, decorators, or configuration

instructions.instruction: Never infer behaviour, key names, or parameter names from conventions or assumptions — if it is not in the docs, do not use it
instructions.original_snippets: Never infer behaviour, key names, or parameter names from conventions or assumptions — if it is not in the docs, do not use it.
instructions.relevant_when: When writing any code that uses PyXLL APIs or configuration
